title: Bad Apples: macOS Native Primitives Abuse Defense

steps.title: Remote Application Scripting for initial execution
steps.text: Attackers use Remote Application Scripting (RAS) to execute commands on remote macOS systems by leveraging Terminal.app as an execution proxy. Base64-encoded payloads are deployed and executed in a two-stage process to bypass Apple's -10016 Handler Error restrictions.
steps.description: Attackers use Remote Application Scripting (RAS) to execute commands on remote macOS systems by leveraging Terminal.app as an execution proxy. Base64-encoded payloads are deployed and executed in a two-stage process to bypass Apple's -10016 Handler Error restrictions.
steps.detectionHints: Monitor for T1072, T1059.002, T1027 indicators

steps.title: AppleScript execution over SSH for lateral movement
steps.text: Adversaries invoke osascript directly over SSH to execute AppleScript commands remotely, using 'do shell script' handlers for arbitrary command execution while bypassing traditional process execution telemetry through Apple Events IPC.
steps.description: Adversaries invoke osascript directly over SSH to execute AppleScript commands remotely, using 'do shell script' handlers for arbitrary command execution while bypassing traditional process execution telemetry through Apple Events IPC.
steps.detectionHints: Monitor for T1021.004, T1059.002, T1570, T1021 indicators

steps.title: Socat remote shell establishment
steps.text: Attackers establish interactive shells using socat to create bidirectional data streams between endpoints, operating entirely outside the SSH ecosystem to avoid SSH-based detection mechanisms and logging.
steps.description: Attackers establish interactive shells using socat to create bidirectional data streams between endpoints, operating entirely outside the SSH ecosystem to avoid SSH-based detection mechanisms and logging.
steps.detectionHints: Monitor for T1021, T1059.004, T1021.004 indicators

steps.title: Finder metadata abuse for payload staging
steps.text: Malicious payloads are stored within Spotlight metadata using the Finder Comment field (kMDItemFinderComment) rather than file contents, bypassing traditional file-based security scanners and static analysis tools that focus on executable code.
steps.description: Malicious payloads are stored within Spotlight metadata using the Finder Comment field (kMDItemFinderComment) rather than file contents, bypassing traditional file-based security scanners and static analysis tools that focus on executable code.

steps.title: Metadata payload extraction and execution
steps.text: Stored payloads are extracted from Spotlight metadata using mdls command, Base64 decoded, and piped directly to bash for execution, allowing malicious code to run from metadata storage locations unexamined by standard EDR solutions.
steps.description: Stored payloads are extracted from Spotlight metadata using mdls command, Base64 decoded, and piped directly to bash for execution, allowing malicious code to run from metadata storage locations unexamined by standard EDR solutions.
steps.detectionHints: Monitor for T1140, T1059.004, T1090.003 indicators

steps.title: LaunchAgent persistence establishment
steps.text: Attackers create LaunchAgent plists in ~/Library/LaunchAgents that execute metadata extraction chains at user login, using osascript to call Finder directly for reading comments and achieving automatic payload execution at startup.
steps.description: Attackers create LaunchAgent plists in ~/Library/LaunchAgents that execute metadata extraction chains at user login, using osascript to call Finder directly for reading comments and achieving automatic payload execution at startup.
